title: Senegal parade Afcon trophy despite title controversy
content: Senegal parade the Africa Cup of Nations trophy before Saturday's friendly match against Peru in Paris - despite being stripped of the title earlier this month.

originalTitle: Spurs have addressed their Tudor error - but cannot afford another
title: Spurs have addressed their Tudor error - but cannot afford another
content: Chief executive Vinai Venkatesham and sporting director Johan Lange must get the next Tottenham appointment correct or else they could follow Igor Tudor, writes Phil McNulty.

originalTitle: BBC Sport experts choose England World Cup XIs - who would you pick?
title: Let's Talk Football Fun! Who Will Play for England?
content:
Hello from Africa!

Hello, wonderful friends! It's your friendly news reporter here, coming to you live from our beautiful continent of Africa! I hope you are all ready for some exciting news today. Guess what we are talking about? FOOTBALL! Yes, the most amazing game in the world! Today, we are going to chat about a big football team called England and a very special tournament. Get ready to learn some cool stuff about how teams get ready for the biggest games!

What is the World Cup?

Imagine a giant football party! The World Cup is just like that, but even bigger! It's a special competition where many, many countries from all over the world send their best football teams to play against each other. They all want to win a shiny, golden trophy! It happens only once every four years. That means you have to wait a long time to see it again! It's super exciting because everyone cheers for their favourite team. Children and grown-ups watch the games together. Did you know? The first ever World Cup was played in 1930! That's a very, very long time ago! It's been happening for almost 100 years!

Meet the England Team!

Today, we are talking about the England football team. England is a country far away, across the big ocean, but they love football just like we do! They have very talented players who wear white jerseys when they play. These players train very hard every day to become strong and fast. They practice kicking the ball, passing the ball, and scoring goals. Playing for your country is a very big honour. All the players want to do their best for England. Did you know? England's team is also called the 'Three Lions' because their badge, which is like their special team picture, has three lions on it!

Who Picks the Players?

Now, how does a team like England get ready for the World Cup? Well, they have a very important person called the 'coach'. The coach is like the team's teacher and captain all in one! His name is Thomas Tuchel, and he is very clever. He watches all the players very carefully. He sees who is fast, who can score goals, and who works well with others. It is his job to choose the very best players for the World Cup. Imagine you had to pick the best players from your school for a big match – it’s a tough job! The coach has to think about everything to make a super strong team. Did you know? Coaches help players learn new tricks and ways to play better together! They teach them special plays and strategies.

What is a 'Squad' and a 'Starting XI'?

The coach first picks a 'squad'. A squad is a group of around 23 to 26 players. These are all the players who will travel together to the World Cup. It's like choosing all the friends you want to invite to a big party, but for football! But on the day of a match, only 11 players can start the game! This special group of 11 players is called the 'Starting XI'. XI is just a fancy way of saying the number eleven! The coach has to think really hard about which 11 players will be the best for that specific game. It's a tricky puzzle! Did you know? Sometimes, players who start on the 'bench' (not in the Starting XI) can come into the game later as a substitute! This means they can help their team if someone gets tired or if the coach wants to try a new plan.

Why Is It Hard to Choose?

Picking the Starting XI is super difficult because there are so many good players! Imagine you have ten delicious fruits, but you can only pick three for your snack. All the fruits are yummy! It's the same with football players. Each player has special skills. Some are fast, some are strong, and some are very good at passing the ball. The coach needs to make sure all the chosen players work together like a happy family. If they don't work together and share the ball, they might not win! Teamwork is super important in football. Everyone needs to help everyone else. Did you know? Football teams often practice 'teamwork drills' to learn how to play well together! They pass the ball to each other and run in special ways to trick the other team.

Meet the Football Experts!

Today's news also talks about 'BBC Sport reporters'. These people are like super fans who know a lot about football! They watch many games and talk to many players and coaches. They are called 'experts' because they understand the game very well. They love to guess who the coach might pick for the Starting XI. It's like they are having their own fun game of 'guess the team'! They share their ideas so everyone can talk about it. Did you know? Many countries have sport reporters who share news about their favourite games! They write in newspapers and talk on TV and radio, just like me!

Your Turn to Be a Coach!

So, now you know how much thought goes into picking a football team. If you were the coach for England, who would you pick for the Starting XI? Would you pick the fastest player? Or the strongest? Or someone who is very good at scoring goals? Think about it! It's fun to imagine you are the coach and pick your dream team! Who would be your captain? Who would score all the goals?

Bye-bye, Football Fans!

Thank you for joining me today, amazing kids! Keep loving football, keep cheering for your favourite teams, and remember that playing together is always the best way to win. Until next time, stay happy and play fair! Bye-bye!
summary: Experts are helping to choose the best players for the England football team to play in the big World Cup tournament, and it's a very tricky job for the coach!
whyMatters: This news matters because the World Cup is a very big football event that everyone loves, and picking the right players is super important for a team to play their best and try to win the shiny trophy!
